response:
## What `src/billing.ts` does

It's the billing domain module. Two functions are named in findings:
- `generateInvoice` (lines 50–253) — a 204-line invoice generator that reads the wall clock heavily (`Date.now()`/`new Date()` at lines 57, 63, 92, 198, 238, 250, 273).
- `calculateRefundPreview` (lines 40–44) — name implies a pure calculation but it calls `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail`, so it actually performs side effects.

The file also encodes (or rather, *doesn't* encode) a permission rule via comment on line 39: *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves"* — the domain terms `owner`, `plan`, `billing` aren't visible nearby in code, so the rule is prose-only. It special-cases the `"enterprise"` plan as a bare string literal (line 7). 5 top-level functions, 310 lines total.

## Files I'd also want to read before editing

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `src/billing.test.ts` | Two weak tests cover this module — `"renders the enterprise plan"` (lines 4–6, zero asserts) and `"creates an invoice"` (lines 8–10, weak matchers). I can't trust either to catch regressions. |
| `src/date.ts` | Likely the intended clock/date helper (also flagged for direct `Date` use). If I'm adding logic, this is where a `now()` abstraction would land. |
| `src/api/plan.ts`, `src/jobs/plan-sync.ts`, `src/ui/pricing.ts` | All four files (incl. billing.ts) hardcode `"enterprise"`. Changing plan logic here likely needs coordinated edits there. |
| `docs/billing.md` | Concept-alias drift: docs mix `tenant`/`account`/`organisation`/`team`/`workspace`. Worth checking which term the billing code is supposed to align with. |

## Risks the scan flagged for `src/billing.ts`

| Severity | Charge | Note |
|---|---|---|
| **high** | God Function (`generateInvoice`, 204 lines, 3.4× threshold) | Sections likely mix responsibilities; localized edits can miss cross-section effects. |
| medium | Temporal Recklessness (7 direct `Date` calls) | Non-deterministic; tests can't pin time without monkey-patching. |
| medium | String Sprinkles (`"enterprise"` in 4 files) | Plan-tier policy duplicated. |
| medium | False Identity (`calculateRefundPreview` does side effects) | Callers may treat as pure. |
| medium | God File (310 lines, just over threshold) | Hidden local coupling. |
| medium | Logic in the Alibi (line 39 comment-rule) | Refund authorization rule lives in prose, not code. |
| low | Unfinished Business (4 TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX between lines 183–308) | Unclear which behaviour is load-bearing. |

Repo-wide context worth knowing before editing: **no `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md`/skills file** (Missing Agent Context, medium), so any conventions I follow have to be inferred from the code itself.
